sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: Following curative-intent surgery, patients should undergo a baseline postoperative CT (chest, abdomen, and pelvis) with contrast and measurement of CA 19-9. If not previously done, genetic testing for inherited mutations, biopsy confirmation, and molecular profiling should be performed. Adjuvant therapy should be initiated within 12 weeks of recovery from surgery. The preferred adjuvant regimen is modified FOLFIRINOX or capecitabine/gemcitabine. The rationale for surveillance is the early detection of recurrence, which may allow for potential enrollment in clinical trials or other forms of treatment. However, data on the effectiveness of surveillance in improving overall survival are limited. Earlier identification of recurrence is a consensus goal. Recurrence after resection can be local (pancreatic bed), regional (lymph nodes), or distant (metastatic). The most common site of recurrence for patients followed for 5 years is the lungs. Patterns of recurrence can be influenced by tumor stage, margin status, and lymph node involvement.

timeline.title: Baseline Postoperative Period
timeline.body: Baseline postoperative CT (chest, abdomen, pelvis) with contrast and CA 19-9 measurement. Genetic testing, biopsy confirmation, and molecular profiling if not done.

timeline.title: Adjuvant Therapy Period
timeline.body: Initiate adjuvant therapy within 12 weeks of recovery. Preferred regimen: modified FOLFIRINOX or capecitabine/gemcitabine.

timeline.title: Surveillance Period
timeline.body: Rationale: early detection of recurrence for potential clinical trials or treatment. Recurrence patterns: local, regional, distant; most common site lungs at 5 years. Influenced by stage, margin, lymph nodes.

late_effects.name: Treatment-related toxicities
late_effects.detail: Monitor for treatment-related toxicities, such as peripheral neuropathy (from oxaliplatin or paclitaxel), renal dysfunction (from cisplatin), and cardiotoxicity (from specific agents).

late_effects.name: Post-pancreatectomy diabetes and exocrine insufficiency
late_effects.detail: Screen for post-pancreatectomy diabetes and exocrine insufficiency, which may require ongoing enzyme replacement.

late_effects.name: Nutritional deficiencies
late_effects.detail: Screen for nutritional deficiencies and malnutrition.

late_effects.name: Psychological distress
late_effects.detail: Monitor for psychological distress, depression, and fatigue.
